code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a monument into a fluent, single‑sentence description.
    Triples sharing the same subject are merged into one clause, and the clauses are
    combined with commas and a final conjunction for better readability.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a short phrase template (uses {obj} placeholder)
    pred_map = {
        "category": "is categorized as {obj}",
        "country": "is located in {obj}",
        "location": "is located at {obj}",
        "municipality": "lies in the municipality of {obj}",
        "state": "lies in the state of {obj}",
        "district": "belongs to the district {obj}",
        "established": "was established on {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "owningOrganisation": "is owned by {obj}",
        "dedicatedTo": "is dedicated to {obj}",
        "designer": "was designed by {obj}",
        "material": "is made of {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "language": "uses the language {obj}",
        "religion": "has religion {obj}",
        "nearestCity": "is near the city of {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "largestCity": "has the largest city {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has the leader title {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "legislature": "has legislature {obj}",
        "hasToItsNorth": "has to its north {obj}",
        "hasToItsSouthwest": "has to its southwest {obj}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west {obj}",
        "hasToItsSoutheast": "has to its southeast {obj}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
    }

    # Helper to clean surrounding quotes from objects
    def clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Group predicates by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        template = pred_map.get(t.predicate, "has {obj}")
        phrase = template.format(obj=clean(t.object))
        grouped[t.subject].append(phrase)

    # Build clauses for each subject
    clauses = []
    for subj, phrases in grouped.items():
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            # join with commas and an "and" before the last item
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + f", and {phrases[-1]}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all clauses into one sentence
    sentence = "; ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
